wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MediRAG 3.0 project overview section focused on the medical AI safety evaluation system. Explain its purpose as a forensic audit layer between RAG pipelines and patient care, architecture overview combining frontend React interface with backend FastAPI services, and relationship to healthcare safety requirements. Document the four-layer audit engine (Faithfulness, Entity Verification, Source Credibility, Contradiction Detection), key features including real-time HRS scoring, document-aware chat, governance dashboard, and federated dataset integration. Include both conceptual overviews for healthcare professionals and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including hallucination risk scoring, target-lock cursor, and verified clinical knowledge bases. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases in clinical decision support and AI governance.

wiki_catalogs.name: FastAPI Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the FastAPI application structure focused on the MediRAG API server architecture. Explain the application initialization, middleware configuration, and lifespan management for model pre-warming. Document the core endpoint handlers including /health, /evaluate, /query, /ingest, and dashboard endpoints. Detail the request/response schema definitions and validation mechanisms. Include practical examples of API usage patterns, error handling strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address CORS configuration, logging setup, and thread-safe operations. Provide code examples demonstrating proper client integration and common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MediRAG 3.0 introduction and purpose section focused on the critical need for medical AI safety evaluation. Explain why blind trust in medical AI is dangerous, the forensic audit layer concept, and how MediRAG-Eval 3.0 addresses hallucination risks in healthcare AI systems. Document the project's mission to prevent AI-related medical errors through automated verification against trusted medical literature. Include the target audience of healthcare professionals, AI developers, and medical institutions. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including hallucination risk scoring, forensic audit, and verified clinical knowledge bases. Provide practical examples demonstrating real-world scenarios where AI hallucinations could cause harm and how the system prevents such outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Evaluation Pipeline Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MediRAG evaluation pipeline orchestration focused on the four-layer audit system architecture. Explain the overall workflow from raw AI responses through individual module processing to aggregated risk assessment. Document the run_evaluation function as the central orchestrator that sequentially executes Faithfulness, Entity Verification, Source Credibility, and Contradiction Detection modules. Detail the evaluation pipeline stages including input preparation, module execution order, result aggregation, and performance timing. Include configuration options for module weights, optional RAGAS evaluation, and RXNorm cache integration. Provide examples of evaluation result interpretation, confidence level assignment, and safety intervention triggers. Document the CLI interface and batch processing capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the React application architecture and build configuration. Detail the Vite-based build system setup, environment variable management, and application entry point configuration. Explain the routing structure, component hierarchy organization, and state management patterns used throughout the application. Document the integration between frontend and backend APIs, including CORS configuration and proxy settings. Cover the development server setup, hot module replacement capabilities, and production optimization strategies. Include guidelines for extending the application structure and adding new routes or components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Health Check Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the /health endpoint focused on system status monitoring and availability checking. Document the GET method with URL pattern /health, response schema HealthResponse containing status and ollama_available fields, and expected response format. Explain the endpoint's role in system health monitoring, how it checks Ollama availability, and integration patterns for deployment health checks. Include practical examples showing successful responses, error scenarios when Ollama is unavailable, and client implementation guidelines for automated health monitoring systems. Address common use cases like Kubernetes readiness probes, Docker container health checks, and microservice dependency verification.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the unit testing framework section. Document the pytest configuration setup, test organization patterns, and mocking strategies for external dependencies. Explain how to test individual evaluation modules in isolation, including Faithfulness, Entity Verification, Source Credibility, and Contradiction Detection modules. Detail the test fixtures and conftest.py configuration for setting up test environments. Include guidelines for testing NLP models, RxNorm API integrations, and FAISS vector operations. Document test data management, mock strategies for expensive ML model loading, and test isolation techniques. Provide examples of testing evaluation pipelines, module scoring functions, and error handling scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Setup and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for MediRAG 3.0 environment setup and configuration focused on system prerequisites, dependency management, and configuration file structure. Document Python environment setup with FastAPI and NLP dependencies, Node.js environment for frontend development, and GPU/CPU optimization settings. Explain the YAML configuration structure including retrieval parameters, model settings, evaluation weights, and API configurations. Detail environment variable requirements, path configurations for data storage, and model download strategies. Include Docker configuration for containerized deployment, volume mounting for persistent data, and network configuration for inter-service communication. Provide step-by-step setup instructions for development and production environments with troubleshooting guidance for common dependency conflicts and version compatibility issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Ingestion
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the document ingestion system focused on dynamic knowledge base management and document processing workflows. Explain the ingestion pipeline from file upload through text extraction, metadata processing, and FAISS index updates. Document supported document formats (PDF, DOCX, TXT), text extraction strategies using PyMuPDF and python-docx, and metadata enrichment processes. Detail the integration with FAISS index management for real-time knowledge base updates, including thread-safe concurrent processing and batch ingestion optimization. Include examples of ingestion workflows for different document types, error handling for corrupted files, and performance considerations for large-scale document processing. Address memory management during bulk ingestion and index rebuilding strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for MediRAG 3.0 focused on rapid deployment and initial setup. Document prerequisites including Python 3.10+, Node.js, and system requirements. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both backend (FastAPI) and frontend (React) components, including virtual environment setup, dependency installation, and environment variable configuration. Include quick start examples for running the development servers, accessing the web interface, and performing basic safety evaluations. Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide verification commands to ensure proper installation. Include initial configuration recommendations for local development versus production deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for configuration management in the MediRAG backend system. Thoroughly explain the YAML configuration structure including logging settings, LLM provider configurations, retrieval parameters, and API settings. Include concrete examples of different deployment scenarios such as local development, GPU-enabled inference, and production environments. Address security considerations for API keys and sensitive configuration data. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common configuration issues and performance tuning recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MediRAG 3.0 key features overview section focused on the five major components that make up the system. Document the four-layer audit engine (Faithfulness Scorer, Mistral 2-Pass Verification, Medical Entity Verifier, Source Credibility Ranking, Contradiction Detection) and explain how each layer contributes to safety evaluation. Detail the MediChat AI interface with real-time HRS scoring, document-aware chat capabilities, app integration console, AI governance dashboard, and federated dataset section. Include both conceptual overviews for stakeholders and technical highlights for developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including target-lock cursor, hallucination risk scoring, and verified clinical knowledge bases. Provide practical examples demonstrating how each feature enhances medical AI safety and compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Faithfulness Scoring Module
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Faithfulness Scoring module focused on DeBERTa-v3 Natural Language Inference (NLI) models for entailment checking. Explain the implementation of sentence-level claim analysis using transformer-based NLI models to verify if generated answers follow logically from provided context. Document the score_faithfulness function parameters including answer text, context documents, and chunk identifiers. Detail the entailment classification system covering entailment, contradiction, and neutral relationships with threshold-based scoring. Include examples of faithfulness evaluation scenarios, handling of long contexts, and fallback mechanisms for edge cases. Document the integration with the broader evaluation pipeline and performance considerations for batch processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Main Chat Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the main chat interface component focused on the real-time medical Q&A experience. Document the chat message handling system, user input processing, and AI response rendering. Explain the HRS (Healthcare Response Score) visualization implementation with color-coded risk indicators and confidence metrics. Detail the document upload integration allowing PDF/DOCX processing and context enhancement. Cover the smart suggestion system with auto-complete functionality and medical query recommendations. Document the patient experience interface with interactive elements and feedback collection. Include state management for chat history, real-time updates, error handling, and user session persistence. Provide implementation examples for extending chat functionality and customizing the conversational flow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Evaluation Endpoint
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the /evaluate endpoint focused on standalone safety evaluation functionality. Document the POST method with URL pattern /evaluate, detailed EvaluateRequest schema including question, answer, context_chunks array, and run_ragas flag, and EvaluateResponse schema with composite_score, hrs, confidence_level, risk_band, and module_results. Explain the evaluation pipeline integration, module scoring system, and risk assessment algorithms. Include comprehensive examples showing request construction with various context chunk configurations, response interpretation with HRS scoring, and error handling for invalid inputs. Document the relationship with individual evaluation modules (faithfulness, entity_verifier, source_credibility, contradiction, ragas), performance considerations for batch processing, and integration patterns for external AI systems requiring safety validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for integration testing focused on API endpoint validation and end-to-end pipeline testing. Document testing strategies for FastAPI endpoints including health checks, evaluation workflows, and query processing. Explain how to test the complete data flow from input validation through evaluation modules to response generation. Detail testing approaches for document ingestion, FAISS index updates, and real-time chat functionality. Include guidelines for testing concurrent requests, error propagation through the pipeline, and performance under load. Document testing patterns for database operations, file upload processing, and external API integrations. Address testing of safety intervention mechanisms and compliance dashboard functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for MediRAG 3.0 deployment strategies focused on production deployment architectures, container orchestration, and cloud platform integration. Document Docker containerization strategies for both backend and frontend services, including multi-stage builds, image optimization, and security hardening. Explain docker-compose configuration for orchestrated deployment, service dependencies, and resource allocation. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the text chunking and preprocessing system focused on optimal context preservation and document segmentation strategies. Explain chunking algorithms including sliding windows, sentence boundary detection, and semantic coherence preservation techniques. Document chunk size optimization for BioBERT embeddings, overlap strategies to maintain context flow, and handling of different document structures (medical literature, clinical notes, research papers). Detail preprocessing steps including text cleaning, special character handling, and metadata attachment. Include examples of chunking strategies for different medical document types, performance optimization for large documents, and quality metrics for chunk coherence assessment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Hybrid Retrieval System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the hybrid retrieval system focused on FAISS semantic search combined with BM25 keyword matching and Reciprocal Rank Fusion algorithms. Detail thread-safe concurrent search operations, lazy index loading, and performance optimization strategies. Include examples of hybrid search queries, result ranking algorithms, and troubleshooting retrieval performance issues.

Provide client implementation guidelines for integrating with external healthcare systems, performance optimization tips for bulk operations, and debugging approaches for API troubleshooting. Address security considerations including input validation, output sanitization, and audit logging requirements.

Explain performance optimization techniques for GPU acceleration, memory management for large models, and scaling considerations for concurrent users. Address network connectivity issues, API rate limiting, and external service dependencies. Provide templates for bug reports, feature requests, and security vulnerability disclosures.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# MediRag 2.0: Comprehensive Project Overview

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
MediRag 2.0 is a sophisticated Medical Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system designed to evaluate the safety and accuracy of AI-generated medical responses. The platform combines advanced natural language processing with clinical knowledge bases to provide comprehensive safety assessments for healthcare-related AI applications.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Safety First**: Implement multi-layered evaluation system to detect hallucinations, contradictions, and unsafe medical claims
- **Clinical Accuracy**: Verify medical entities, dosages, and treatment recommendations against authoritative sources
- **Transparency**: Provide detailed explanations of evaluation results with source citations and confidence metrics
- **Real-time Assessment**: Enable live evaluation of AI responses during clinical decision-making processes

### Target Audience
- Healthcare professionals seeking to validate AI-generated medical information
- AI developers building clinical decision support systems
- Medical institutions implementing AI safety protocols
- Researchers studying AI safety in healthcare contexts

## Technical Architecture

### System Overview
The MediRag 2.0 architecture follows a microservice-based design with clear separation between frontend, backend, and data processing layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[FastAPI Backend]
    B --> C[Retrieval Engine]
    B --> D[Evaluation Modules]
    B --> E[LLM Integration]
    
    C --> F[FAISS Index]
    C --> G[BioBERT Embeddings]
    
    D --> H[Faithfulness Module]
    D --> I[Entity Verifier]
    D --> J[Source Credibility]
    D --> K[Contradiction Detector]
    D --> L[RAGAS Evaluator]
    
    E --> M[Mistral AI]
    E --> N[Ollama Local]
    E --> O[OpenAI/Gemini]
    
    F --> P[PubMed Dataset]
    G --> Q[DrugBank Database]
```

### Component Breakdown

#### Backend Services
The backend is built on FastAPI with modular architecture supporting:
- **RESTful API Endpoints**: `/health`, `/evaluate`, `/query`, `/ingest`
- **Real-time Evaluation**: Live assessment of AI-generated responses
- **Thread-safe Operations**: Concurrent document ingestion and retrieval
- **Audit Logging**: Complete tracking of all evaluation activities

#### Frontend Interface
The React-based frontend provides:
- **Interactive Chat Interface**: Real-time conversation with safety evaluation
- **Visual Risk Indicators**: Color-coded safety badges and risk gauges
- **Source Transparency**: Detailed citation displays with relevance scores
- **Configuration Panel**: Customizable evaluation parameters

#### Data Pipeline
The system processes medical literature through:
- **Document Ingestion**: PubMedQA and MedQA-USMLE datasets
- **Semantic Chunking**: Context-aware text segmentation
- **Vector Embedding**: BioBERT-based semantic indexing
- **Hybrid Retrieval**: FAISS + BM25 keyword search fusion

### Design Patterns
- **Pipeline Pattern**: Sequential processing of documents through ingestion → chunking → embedding → indexing
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable evaluation modules with standardized interfaces
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time monitoring and alerting for safety violations
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic LLM provider selection and configuration

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

#### Backend API (`Backend/src/api/main.py`)
The primary FastAPI application serving:
- **Health Monitoring**: System status and dependency checks
- **End-to-End Pipeline**: Complete question-answering with safety evaluation
- **Dynamic Ingestion**: Real-time document addition to knowledge base
- **Dashboard Integration**: Historical evaluation data and statistics

#### Evaluation Orchestrator (`Backend/src/evaluate.py`)
Central coordination of all safety evaluation modules:
- **Module Orchestration**: Sequential execution of evaluation components
- **Result Aggregation**: Weighted combination of safety metrics
- **Performance Tracking**: Latency measurement across all components
- **Error Handling**: Graceful degradation for unavailable modules

### Core Modules

#### Faithfulness Module (`Backend/src/modules/faithfulness.py`)
- **NLI-Based Verification**: Uses cross-encoder models to detect entailment
- **Claim-Level Analysis**: Individual sentence verification against evidence
- **Threshold-Based Classification**: ENTAILED/NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED scoring
- **Batch Processing**: Optimized inference for multiple claim-context pairs

#### Entity Verification (`Backend/src/modules/entity_verifier.py`)
- **Clinical Entity Recognition**: Identifies drugs, dosages, conditions, procedures
- **RxNorm Integration**: Pharmaceutical terminology validation
- **Dosage Verification**: Numerical accuracy checking with tolerance thresholds
- **Critical Risk Assessment**: Severity classification for safety violations

#### Source Credibility (`Backend/src/modules/source_credibility.py`)
- **Evidence Tier Scoring**: Journal quality and publication type assessment
- **Metadata Analysis**: Source reliability and peer-review status evaluation
- **Weighted Scoring**: Hierarchical importance assignment to different publication types
- **Keyword Matching**: Evidence-based source validation

#### Contradiction Detection (`Backend/src/modules/contradiction.py`)
- **Sentence Pair Analysis**: Inter-sentence consistency checking
- **Cross-Encoder Validation**: Model-based contradiction identification
- **Adaptive Thresholds**: Context-aware contradiction severity assessment
- **Pairwise Comparison**: Efficient sentence-to-sentence comparison algorithms

### Configuration Approach

#### Central Configuration (`Backend/config.yaml`)
Comprehensive system configuration including:
- **Retrieval Parameters**: Top-k selection, chunk sizes, embedding models
- **Module Settings**: Model names, thresholds, batch sizes for each evaluation component
- **LLM Integration**: Provider configurations, API keys, temperature settings
- **Logging & Metrics**: Audit trail configuration and performance monitoring

#### Frontend Environment (`Frontend/package.json`)
React application dependencies:
- **UI Framework**: React 19 with modern hooks and state management
- **Routing**: React Router for navigation between different sections
- **Styling**: CSS-in-JS with responsive design principles
- **Animation**: GSAP for smooth user interface transitions

### External Dependencies

#### Backend Dependencies (`Backend/requirements.txt`)
- **NLP Libraries**: LangChain, Transformers, Sentence-Transformers
- **Vector Search**: FAISS for efficient similarity search
- **Medical Databases**: RxNorm API integration for pharmaceutical validation
- **Web Framework**: FastAPI with Uvicorn ASGI server
- **Data Processing**: Pandas, NumPy, PyArrow for efficient data manipulation

#### Frontend Dependencies (`Frontend/package.json`)
- **Core Framework**: React 19 with functional components and hooks
- **UI Components**: Custom-designed medical-themed interface
- **Markdown Support**: React Markdown for formatted medical content
- **Animation Library**: GSAP for interactive user experience elements

### Integration Points

#### API Endpoints
- **Health Check**: `/health` - System status and dependency validation
- **Evaluation API**: `/evaluate` - Standalone safety assessment
- **Query Pipeline**: `/query` - Complete end-to-end evaluation
- **Document Ingestion**: `/ingest` - Dynamic knowledge base updates
- **File Parsing**: `/parse_file` - Document preprocessing for uploads

#### Database Integration
- **SQLite Audit Logs**: Persistent storage of evaluation history
- **FAISS Index Storage**: Vector similarity search capabilities
- **Metadata Management**: Chunk-level information tracking
- **Configuration Persistence**: Runtime parameter storage

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[User Query] --> B[Retriever]
    B --> C[LLM Generator]
    C --> D[Evaluation Pipeline]
    
    D --> E[Faithfulness]
    D --> F[Entity Verification]
    D --> G[Source Credibility]
    D --> H[Contradiction Detection]
    D --> I[RAGAS]
    
    E --> J[Aggregator]
    F --> J
    G --> J
    H --> J
    I --> J
    
    J --> K[Composite Score]
    K --> L[Health Risk Score]
    L --> M[Safety Intervention]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Multi-Layer Safety Evaluation
The system implements four independent safety evaluation modules working in concert:

**Faithfulness Assessment**: Detects whether AI responses are supported by retrieved evidence through natural language inference models.

**Entity Verification**: Validates clinical entities against authoritative medical databases with dosage accuracy checking.

**Source Credibility**: Rates evidence quality based on publication type and journal reputation.

**Contradiction Detection**: Identifies inconsistencies within AI responses and between responses and evidence.

#### Intelligent Safety Intervention
Automated response modification system:
- **Critical Block**: Prevents unsafe responses with HRS ≥ 86
- **High-Risk Regeneration**: Automatically regenerates responses with stricter prompts
- **Transparent Communication**: Clear indication of safety interventions to users

#### Real-Time Document Processing
Dynamic knowledge base management:
- **PDF/DOCX Parsing**: Automatic extraction from uploaded medical documents
- **Semantic Chunking**: Context-aware text segmentation for optimal retrieval
- **Vector Embedding**: BioBERT-based semantic indexing for similarity search
- **Atomic Updates**: Thread-safe index modifications preventing data corruption

### Implementation Highlights

#### Performance Optimization (`Backend/src/pipeline/retriever.py`)
Advanced hybrid retrieval combining:
- **FAISS Semantic Search**: High-dimensional vector similarity
- **BM25 Keyword Search**: Traditional information retrieval
- **Reciprocal Rank Fusion**: Optimal combination of both approaches
- **Lazy Loading**: On-demand model initialization for efficiency

#### Modular Architecture (`Backend/src/modules/`)
Clean separation of concerns:
- **Standardized Interfaces**: Consistent EvalResult data structure across modules
- **Graceful Degradation**: Unavailable modules don't block overall evaluation
- **Performance Monitoring**: Built-in latency tracking for optimization
- **Extensible Design**: Easy addition of new evaluation criteria

#### User Experience (`Frontend/src/pages/MediChat.jsx`)
Sophisticated chat interface:
- **Risk Visualization**: Color-coded safety indicators with real-time updates
- **Source Transparency**: Detailed citation display with relevance scoring
- **Interactive Controls**: Adjustable evaluation parameters and model settings
- **Document Integration**: Seamless medical document upload and analysis

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> QueryReceived
    QueryReceived --> DocumentParsing: File Upload
    QueryReceived --> SemanticSearch: Text Query
    DocumentParsing --> SemanticSearch
    SemanticSearch --> LLMGeneration
    LLMGeneration --> SafetyEvaluation
    SafetyEvaluation --> RiskAssessment
    RiskAssessment --> InterventionCheck: HRS Calculation
    InterventionCheck --> SafeResponse: HRS < 40
    InterventionCheck --> HighRiskRegeneration: 40 ≤ HRS < 86
    InterventionCheck --> CriticalBlock: HRS ≥ 86
    HighRiskRegeneration --> SafetyEvaluation
    CriticalBlock --> [*]
    SafeResponse --> [*]
```

The MediRag 2.0 system represents a comprehensive solution for ensuring AI safety in healthcare applications, combining cutting-edge NLP techniques with rigorous clinical validation standards to provide trustworthy medical AI assistance.

**Sources:**
- [requirements.txt](Backend/requirements.txt)
- [config.yaml](Backend/config.yaml)
- [main.py](Backend/src/api/main.py)
- [evaluate.py](Backend/src/evaluate.py)
- [faithfulness.py](Backend/src/modules/faithfulness.py)
- [retriever.py](Backend/src/pipeline/retriever.py)
- [base.py](Backend/src/modules/base.py)
- [__init__.py](Backend/src/modules/__init__.py)
- [aggregator.py](Backend/src/evaluation/aggregator.py)
- [ingest.py](Backend/src/pipeline/ingest.py)
- [package.json](Frontend/package.json)
- [App.jsx](Frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [vite.config.js](Frontend/vite.config.js)
- [MediChat.jsx](Frontend/src/pages/MediChat.jsx)

</blog>
